title: HTML Charset
content:
### HTML Character Sets A **charset** (character set) defines how characters are encoded into bytes. --- ### The `` Tag ```html ``` This should be the **first** element in ``. --- ### Common Character Sets | Charset | Description | Characters | |---------|-------------|------------| | **ASCII** | Basic English | 128 chars | | **ANSI** (Windows-1252) | Western languages | 256 chars | | **ISO-8859-1** | Western European | 256 chars | | **UTF-8** | All languages (recommended) | 1M+ chars | | **UTF-16** | Unicode with 2+ bytes | 1M+ chars | --- ### Why UTF-8? | Feature | Benefit | |---------|---------| | **Universal** | Supports all languages | | **Emoji** | Every emoji is supported | | **Backward compatible** | Works with ASCII | | **Web standard** | Used by 98%+ of websites | --- ### Charset Differences ```html

日本語 中文 العربية

Emojis: 😀 🎉 ❤️

Symbols: ∑ ∫ ∞

``` --- ### Specifying Charset in HTTP The server can also set charset via HTTP header: ``` Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 ``` --- > **Best Practice:** Always use `UTF-8` for new websites. Include `` as the first element in ``. Never use ASCII or ANSI for modern web development.
codeExample:
UTF-8 Characters

English: Hello World

Arabic: مرحبا بالعالم

Japanese: こんにちは

Chinese: 你好世界

Russian: Привет мир

Emojis: 😂 ❤️ 👍 🎉

quiz.question: Which charset is recommended for modern HTML documents?
